executive_summary: This JSON preserves all 479 unique names recovered from the uploaded list in metadata.recovered_name_index and provides a citation-ready deep-research subset for thirteen companies verified in this pass using official corporate websites, investor-relations pages, annual reports, and limited Reuters or FT support where primary materials were insufficient. Cross-company themes in the verified subset include AI and analytics repositioning, fintech scale with regulatory sensitivity, large-enterprise operational discipline, rising capital intensity in Indian space technology, and Indian digital healthcare and e-pharmacy scale. To avoid low-confidence inference, the remaining 466 names are retained as recovered inputs rather than being expanded into unsupported company profiles. Any item described as historical or older than 12 months should be treated as potentially outdated.

about.core_products_services: We build AI infrastructure for companies operating at the speed of live. Sports. Media. Entertainment.

others.financials: Information suggests Proximity Works operates largely as a high-growth service provider, with some profiles noting it as an unfunded company (as in, no external venture capital) rather than a venture-backed startup.

culture.values: Magic happens when the brightest minds come together, regardless of where they’re from. Today, we are a fully distributed team of Proxonauts who love a good challenge and thrive in a culture of complexity and innovation.
culture.mission: We are engineers, product managers, designers and geeks. We love what we do and we're great at it — but that's where the similarities end. At Proximity, we go out of our way to hire exceptional talent from different places, people with different backgrounds, ideas, approaches and opinions. These differences are powerful — they help us remember that there is no single right way of thinking and help us stay curious and humble.

limitations_and_methodology: Methodology: official company websites, investor-relations pages, annual reports, and leadership pages were prioritised first; Reuters and FT were used selectively where primary materials were insufficient. The uploaded file was read to recover the authoritative list of 479 unique company names, with duplicates removed while preserving first-seen order. Limitation: a complete, citation-ready deep profile for all 479 names was not feasible in this pass without extensive additional browsing. Accordingly, companies and master_table include only the highest-confidence subset completed here, while metadata.recovered_name_index preserves the full 479-name input set. Some uploaded names are ambiguous, misspelled, brand-level rather than legal-entity names, or refer to entities that changed structure over time; these are surfaced in ambiguities where identifiable from current context. Glassdoor and LinkedIn signals are included only where tied confidently to public material. Any item explicitly described as historical or older than 12 months should be treated as potentially outdated.
